attributes.description: Kernel-level telemetry for game engine optimisation: GPU pipeline latency, CPU scheduler health, I/O and futex contention, stutter scoring. Essential for engine developers, driver authors, and performance engineers.

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_fence.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU Fence µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_fence.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_fence.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU Fence µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_fence.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_sched.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU Sched µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_sched.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_sched.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU Sched µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.gpu_sched.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.runqueue.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `RQ µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.runqueue.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.runqueue.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `RQ µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.runqueue.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.bio.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `I/O µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.bio.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.bio.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `I/O µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.bio.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.futex.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `Futex µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.futex.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.futex.latency_avg_us` IS NOT NULL | STATS `Futex µs` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.futex.latency_avg_us`), 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.stutter.severity_score` IS NOT NULL | STATS `Stutter Score` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.stutter.severity_score`), 2)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.ebpf-default | WHERE `gamepulse.ebpf.stutter.severity_score` IS NOT NULL | STATS `Stutter Score` = ROUND(AVG(`gamepulse.ebpf.stutter.severity_score`), 2)

embeddableConfig.title: GPU Pipeline Latency Over Time — GPU fence latency = time from command submission to GPU completion (measured by DRM fence callbacks). GPU scheduler latency = time GPU work spends queued waiting for execution. Spikes indicate GPU ring buffer saturation or driver overhead.

CPU Scheduler Latency Over Time — Runqueue latency is the time game threads spend waiting for a CPU core. High values (>200 µs) indicate CPU saturation, conflicting affinity, or SMT interference. CCX migrations = cross-chiplet scheduler migrations on AMD Zen (expensive cache miss).
CCX Cross-Node Migrations — Thread migrations across AMD CCX chiplets cause cache misses. Elevated counts correlate with microstutter in CPU-bound scenarios.
Block I/O Latency Over Time — High I/O latency during gameplay indicates shader cache compilation I/O or asset streaming bottleneck. Correlate with FPS drops on the Game Performance dashboard.

Futex Contention Over Time — Futex wait counts reflect thread synchronisation overhead. Game engines use futexes for job system barriers; high counts can indicate unbalanced work distribution between render and update threads.

Stutter Severity Score Over Time — Composite score from frame drops + scheduler misses + I/O spikes. Score > 0.3 is noticeable; > 0.6 is severe. Correlate spikes with GPU pipeline and runqueue panels above.
Engine Profile per Session — graphics API, upscaler, and eBPF summary

attributes.title: GamePulse — Game Engine
